```{pyodide-python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show simulation code"
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: false


import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from io import BytesIO
import base64
from js import window
import urllib.parse

# -- Dummy data setup (replace with your real variables) --
import numpy as np

ngens, ti, tr, n_pops = 50, 1, 50, 10
mean_fitness_ensemble = np.random.rand(tr, n_pops)
mean_fit_mean = np.mean(mean_fitness_ensemble, axis=1)
cov_ensemble = np.random.rand(tr, n_pops)
cov_mean = np.mean(cov_ensemble, axis=1)
xx = [x / ti for x in range(ngens * ti)]

# -- Create plot --
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))

plt.subplot(1, 2, 1)
for i in range(n_pops):
    plt.plot(xx[:tr], mean_fitness_ensemble[:tr, i], color='gray', alpha=0.3)
plt.plot(xx[:tr], mean_fit_mean[:tr], color='red', lw=2, label='Mean trajectory')
plt.xlabel('Generation')
plt.ylabel('Mean fitness')
plt.title('Mean fitness trajectories')
plt.legend()

plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)
for i in range(n_pops):
    plt.plot(xx[:tr], cov_ensemble[:tr, i], color='gray', alpha=0.3)
plt.plot(xx[:tr], cov_mean[:tr], color='blue', lw=2, label='Mean trajectory')
plt.xlabel('Generation')
plt.ylabel('cov(X,Y)')
plt.title('Covariance trajectories')
plt.legend()

plt.tight_layout()

# -- Convert to PNG base64 --
buf = BytesIO()
fig.savefig(buf, format="png", bbox_inches='tight')
buf.seek(0)
b64_image = base64.b64encode(buf.read()).decode("utf-8")
buf.close()

# -- Create HTML popup content --
html = f"""
<html>
<head>
  <title>Trajectory Plot</title>
</head>
<body style="margin:0; padding:0; text-align:center; font-family:sans-serif;">
  <button onclick="download()" style="margin:10px; padding:8px 16px; font-size:14px;">💾 Save as PNG</button><br>
  <img id="plotimg" src="data:image/png;base64,{b64_image}" style="max-width:100%;">
  <script>
    function download() {{
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = document.getElementById('plotimg').src;
      a.download = 'plot.png';
      a.click();
    }}
  </script>
</body>
</html>
"""

# -- Encode HTML and open it in new popup safely --
html_bytes = html.encode('utf-8')
html_b64 = base64.b64encode(html_bytes).decode('utf-8')
url_safe = urllib.parse.quote(html_b64)
window.open(f"data:text/html;base64,{url_safe}", "_blank")
